chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.D-176 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A" 2. For hearing of main case. ------ 09.10.2025 M/s. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani and Kiran Manzoor, Advocates for the Petitioners. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, Additional Advocate General, Sindh a/w Aurangzeb Samo, Regional Director, Local Government Department, Larkana, Mumtaz Ali Junejo, Additional Director, Local Government, Shikarpur, Rashid Rehman, Assistant Director, Local Government Department, District Larkana, Sikdar Ali Shah, Chairman, Union Council Nau Wah, Taluka Lakhi and Bhural, U.C. Secretary. ------ Pursuant to Court’s notice, Sikandar Ali Shah, Chairman and Bhural, Secretary, Union Council, Nau Wah, Taluka Lakhi are present and they have submitted eight cheques bearing Cheque No.52259022 amounting to Rs.86953/-, 52259023 amounting to Rs.86953/-, 52259024 amounting to Rs.86953/-, 52259025 amounting to Rs.86953/-, 52259026 amounting to Rs.88695/-, 52259027 amounting to Rs.88695/-, 52259028 amounting to Rs.88695/- and 52259029 amounting to Rs.88693/- with regard to the salaries of the petitioners, take on regard. The Accountant of this Court is directed to hand over the same to the petitioners upon proper identification. Mumtaz Ali Junejo, the Additional Director, Local Government, Shikarpur, present, submits that he has recommended the Secretary Local Government Department for transfer of the petitioners to any other place, as their villages are located far away from the place of their postings and thus cannot reach the duty place in time.

The Accountant of this Court is directed to hand over the same to the petitioners upon proper identification. Mumtaz Ali Junejo, the Additional Director, Local Government, Shikarpur, present, submits that he has recommended the Secretary Local Government Department for transfer of the petitioners to any other place, as their villages are located far away from the place of their postings and thus cannot reach the duty place in time. In the given circumstances, as the petitioners are low paid employees and cannot afford the traveling expenses to attend their jobs, therefore, the Secretary, Local Government Department is directed to decide the representations made by the Additional Director, Local Government Department within one month’s time and relocate the petitioners to any other suitable place. The petitioners are warned that if they remained absent from their duties, they shall not be entitled for their salaries. Learned counsel, being satisfied, seeks disposal of instant petition, which is not opposed by the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Order accordingly. Judge Judge Manzoor
